original: Tyrion: With no way to protect themselves,[p/] this kingdom was on the brink of destruction.
translation: Tyrion: 因为没有保卫自身的方法，[p/]王国处于毁灭的边缘。

original: Tyrion: Its people prayed to Bahamut:[p/] the God of Dragons,[p/] for salvation.
translation: Tyrion: 国民只能向巴哈姆特，[p/]也就是巨龙之神祈祷，[p/]祈望他带来救赎。

original: Tyrion: And almost as if to answer their prayers,[p/] Rathallion—[p/]the last of the ancient dragons—[p/]appeared before them.
translation: Tyrion: 然后就像是回应了他们的祈祷，[p/]拉萨利昂，[p/]这尊末代古龙，[p/]真的现身在他们面前。

original: Tyrion: He took pity on their plight and granted them the gift of magic.
translation: Tyrion: 祂同情他们的困境，于是赐予他们魔法。

original: Tyrion: It was a weapon our greatest warriors wielded without equal.
translation: Tyrion: 这是我们最伟大的战士们所挥舞的、无与伦比的武器。

original: Tyrion: With arcane magic in hand,[p/] these warriors laid devastation upon our enemies.
translation: Tyrion: 拥有了奥术魔法，[p/]战士们向敌人带去了毁灭性的打击。

original: Tyrion: They didn't just stop the war.[p/] They joined the battle and singlehandedly won it,[p/] taking large swathes of territory for themselves.
translation: Tyrion: 他们不仅仅是中止了战争。[p/]他们加入后轻而易举地赢得战斗，[p/]为自己获取大片的领土。

original: Tyrion: With our nation protected and our power made known,[p/] these warriors went on to form the noble houses of today.
translation: Tyrion: 随着国家受到保护，武力得到彰显，[p/]这些战士转而组建了流传至今的贵族阶层。

original: Ruby: Hmph...[p/] What a load of nonsense.
translation: Ruby: 哼……[p/]一大堆胡言乱语……

original: Ruby: That story is nothing but religious propaganda created to prop up the noble houses.
translation: Ruby: 这种故事只不过是赞扬贵族的宣传手段。

original: ERIS: Regardless,[p/] the significance of this legend is in the Scaled Lord himself.
translation: ERIS: 无论如何，[p/]这个传说的意义在于鳞甲领主本身。

original: TYRION: Rathallion?
translation: Tyrion: 拉萨利昂？

original: Eris: He bestowed the power to become a mage on several individuals.
translation: Eris: 它将魔力赋予几个人，让他们成为法师。

original: Eris: And to this day,[p/] that power can only be passed through bloodlines.
translation: Eris: 而直到今日，[p/]这种力量也只能通过血脉传承。

original: Eris: Can you think of an arcane spell that could reproduce something like that?
translation: Eris: 你能想到有什么奥术魔法能做到这一点吗？

original: Tyrion: No...[p/] I suppose not.
translation: Tyrion: 不……[p/]我想是没有的。

original: Eris: Rathallion had powers that weren’t bound by the conventions of the Arcane Arts.
translation: Eris: 拉萨利昂有某种不受奥术魔法的常规限制的力量。

original: Eris: In other words,[p/] he could use magic that was distinct from the magic of today.
translation: Eris: 换句话来说，[p/]他可以使用与现时我们熟知的奥术不同的魔法。

original: Ruby: Please,[p/] do you honestly expect us to believe a story like that?
translation: Ruby: 拜托了，[p/]你真的觉得我们会相信这样的故事吗？

original: Ruby: It’s completely unrealistic.
translation: Ruby: 这根本是不现实的。

original: Eris: ...As unrealistic as a non-mage using magic?
translation: Eris: ……就像一个并非法师的人使用魔法一样不现实吗？

original: She had a point...[p/] It was the closest thing I had to a lead right now.
translation: 她说得有道理……[p/]这是我目前能找到最接近的线索了。

original: Eris: You're already resorting to PRESENTING me things?
translation: Eris: 你已经开始要对我<size=1.5em>出示</size>东西了吗？

original: Eris: We've only just started.[p/] I really think you're overthinking things.
translation: Eris: 我们才刚开始呢，[p/]我觉得你想太复杂了。

original: Eris: Or do you simply want to see all the unique things that I have to say?
translation: Eris: 或者你只是想看我所有能说的独特内容？

original: Eris: I'm sorry to disappoint you,[p/] but I'm not some script for you to toy around with.
translation: Eris: 不好意思让你失望了，[p/]但我可不是什么你能玩弄的台本哦。

original: Eris: Feel free to try again later, though.
translation: Eris: 不过等会儿可以随你尝试啦。

original: Catherine: Ah...[p/] Of course, it's [i]you[/i] lot making all of this noise.
translation: Catherine: 啊……[p/]果然呢，是[i]你们[/i]在不断制造噪音。

original: One of the instructors of the Academy approached us disdainfully.
translation: 学院的一位教员不屑地走近我们。

original: She looked like a woman of the aristocracy.[p/] Every bit of her demeanor carried that—[p/]from the stiffness of her posture to the condescending grimace on her face.
translation: 她看起来像是一位贵族女子。[p/]从她强硬的姿势到居高临下的表情，[p/]她的言行举止无不透露着这一点。

original: She glared at each of us like we were the most disgusting things she'd ever seen in her life.
translation: 她用一种好似在看此生所见最恶心之事的眼神打量着我们每一个人。

original: WALLACE: P-[p/][s/]P-[p/][s/]Professor Bellwether!
translation: WALLACE: 贝、[p/][s/]贝、[p/][s/]贝尔维瑟教授！

original: CATHERINE: That is [s/][i]Professor[/i][p/] Bellwether to you.
translation: CATHERINE: 你应该称呼我贝尔维瑟[i]教授[/i]。

original: Celeste: But he just called you—
translation: Celeste: 可他刚刚不就是这样称呼你——

original: CATHERINE: I knew letting you rabble into our walls would lead to nothing but trouble.
translation: CATHERINE: 我就知道你们这些贱民混进来只会制造麻烦。

original: CATHERINE: ...You’ve even started associating with our malcontents.
translation: CATHERINE: ……你们甚至已经开始和我们这里的问题分子搭上线了。

original: CATHERINE: Wallace.
translation: CATHERINE: 华莱士。

original: WALLACE: Y-[p/]y-[p/]y-[p/]y-[p/]yes?!
translation: WALLACE: 我、[p/]我、[p/]我、[p/]我、[p/]我在？！

original: CATHERINE: How am I supposed to understand you when you’re stuttering?
translation: CATHERINE: 你这么结结巴巴的我怎么知道你在说什么？！

original: WALLACE: Y-[p/][s/]Yes,[p/][s/] Professor Bellwether!
translation: WALLACE: 是、[p/][s/]是，[p/][s/]贝尔维瑟教授！

original: CATHERINE: Straighten out your uniform and fix that [i]awful[/i] hair.
translation: CATHERINE: 整理好你的制服和那头[i]乱糟糟[/i]的头发。

original: CATHERINE: I know it’s unreasonable to expect anything more from you,[p/] but try not to look like such a street urchin.
translation: CATHERINE: 我知道对你有任何期待都是徒劳的，[p/]但至少别让自己看着像个街头流浪的小子。

original: Wallace shrank away from the professor.[p/] He reacted like a scared and beaten animal.
translation: 华莱士从教授身边畏缩地退开，[p/]就像被痛击而受了惊吓的小动物。

original: I could see Celeste's fist start to clench.[p/] She looked like she was about to toss the professor across the room.
translation: 我看到塞莱斯特的拳头逐渐握紧，[p/]她看着就像要把教授扔到房间的另一头去。

original: I flashed her a look.[p/] Any trouble we caused would just make things worse for Eris and Wallace.
translation: 我赶紧向她使眼色。[p/]我们惹下的每个问题都只会让厄莉斯和华莱士的处境更艰难。

original: Catherine: As for the rest of you,[p/] your rowdy behavior is disrupting the other students.
translation: Catherine: 至于其他人，[p/]你们的吵闹行为正在干扰其他学生。

original: Catherine: Leave.[p/] Now.
translation: Catherine: 出去，[p/]马上。

original: RUBY: Oh, please.[p/] The headmaster gave us express permission to be here.
translation: RUBY: 哦，拜托。[p/]是校长许可我们进入这里的。

original: Catherine: That may be,[p/] but the school board has placed the Imperial Library under my jurisdiction.
translation: Catherine: 可能是这样，[p/]但学院董事会已经决议将帝国图书馆划归我来管理。

original: Catherine: I can remove anyone who disrupts the studies of our prized students.
translation: Catherine: 我可以驱逐任何干扰优秀学生们学习的人。

original: Ruby: Why would [i]you[/i] have authority over the library?
translation: Ruby: [i]你[/i]怎么会有管理图书馆的权力？

original: Tyrion: My apologies, Professor.[p/] We'll try to keep our volume lower.
translation: Tyrion: 非常抱歉，教授。[p/]我们会尽量压低声音。

original: Catherine: I don't believe I was talking to you,[p/][s/] [i]boy[/i].[p/] Know your place.
translation: Catherine: 我可不认为自己有与你说话，[p/][s/][i]小子[/i]。[p/]摆清自己的位置。

original: Celeste: Oh, Tyrion.[p/] [i]Please[/i] tell me you feel threatened so I can "defend" you.
translation: Celeste: 哎呀，提利昂。[p/][i]请[/i]告诉我你正遭受威胁，这样我就能“保护”你了。

original: I wanted to avoid a confrontation,[p/] but a part of me really wanted to see that.
translation: 我试图避免正面冲突，[p/]但我心里也有点想看她那么做。

original: Catherine: If you refuse to leave,[p/] I'll have no choice but to remove you myself.
translation: Catherine: 如果你们拒绝自行离开，[p/]我就只能自己动手了。

original: Catherine: I'll have you know that I'm the most accomplished mage at this academy.
translation: Catherine: 我会让你们知道本人正是学院里最卓越的法师。

original: Celeste: Trust me.[p/] You'll find spellcasting difficult while something's hitting you in the face. [h="Come at me, bitch.|I'll wear you like a sweater." e="ha"/]
translation: Celeste: 信我。[p/]当有人一拳打在你脸上时，你会发现施放魔法是一件多么困难的事情。 [h="来啊，贱人。|看我秒杀你。" e="ha"/]

original: ...I definitely caught hints of Flinhart in her thoughts every so often.
translation: ……我时不时在她的思绪中捕捉到弗林哈特的影子。

original: CATHERINE: H-[p/][s/]Hmph...[p/] I don't have to stand for this. [h="That girl is like a rabid dog...|It'd be best to keep my distance." e="f" /]
translation: CATHERINE: 咳、[p/][s/]哼…….[p/]我不必在这里忍受这个。 [h="那个女孩像条疯狗一样……|最好离她远一点。" e="f" /]

original: Catherine: I was just on my way to speak with the headmaster.[p/] He [i]will[/i] be hearing of this.
translation: Catherine: 我正好就要去和校长谈谈。[p/]他[i]会l[/i]听闻这一切的。

original: Celeste: Oh,[p/][s/] you're going to tell on us?[p/][s/] I'm [i]so[/i] scared!
translation: Celeste: 噢？[p/][s/]你想告我们的状吗？[p/][s/]我[i]好[/i]害怕哦！

original: WALLACE: ...Celeste,[p/] you're a little scary.
translation: WALLACE: ……塞莱斯特，[p/]你有点吓人了。

original: Celeste: Aw...[p/] Thank you.
translation: Celeste: 喔……[p/]谢啦。

original: ERIS: Anyways,[p/] Wallace.[p/] You’ve finished this week’s assignment, haven’t you?
translation: ERIS: 算啦。[p/] 华莱士，[p/]你这周作业都做完了对吧？

original: Eris: Would you mind entertaining Celeste while I aid Mr. Cuthbert?
translation: Eris: 在我帮卡斯伯特先生研究的时候，你介意招待一下塞莱斯特吗？

original: WALLACE: U-[p/][s/]Um...[p/] I don’t know...
translation: WALLACE: 呃、[p/][s/]嗯……[p/]我不知道……

original: CELESTE: Now that you mention it...[p/] I could really use some help learning a new spell formula.
translation: CELESTE: 既然你都这么说了……[p/]我确实需要人帮忙找一个具体的魔法范式。

original: CELESTE: You know your way around here.[p/] Right, Wallace?
translation: CELESTE: 你对这里很熟悉吧。[p/]是不，华莱士？

original: WALLACE: U-[p/][s/]Um...[p/] O-[p/][s/]okay.
translation: WALLACE: 呃、[p/][s/]嗯……[p/]好、[p/][s/]好的。

original: RUBY: ...
translation: RUBY: ……

original: TYRION: I’ll be discreet, Ms. Tymora.
translation: TYRION: 我会小心的，泰摩拉女士。

original: RUBY: ...Please do.
translation: RUBY: ……拜托你一定要这么做。

original: RUBY: I still have some business to take care of here.
translation: RUBY: 我在这里还有其他事情要处理。

original: Ruby: I'll leave you to it.
translation: Ruby: 接下来都靠你们自己了。

original: She was about to leave,[p/] but she stared at Eris warily.
translation: 她正要离开的时候，[p/]转头警惕地盯着厄莉斯。

original: Eris: ...Is something wrong, Ruby?
translation: Eris: ……有什么问题吗，鲁比？

original: Ruby: No.[p/] Not at all.
translation: Ruby: 不，[p/]没有任何问题。

original: I spent the next few hours searching through the library’s books with Eris.
translation: 接下来几个小时，我与厄莉斯一起在图书馆搜寻资料。

original: I hadn’t thought to look into the ancient dragons.[p/] The library had religious texts about them in abundance.
translation: 我之前没想过要调查那些古龙，[p/]刚好图书馆里有很多关于他们的宗教典籍。

original: There was one problem, though...
translation: 虽说还是有一个问题……

original: TYRION: These texts read more like myths than real historical documents.
translation: TYRION: 这些文本读起来与其说是历史记录，更像是神话。

original: ERIS: It can’t be helped.[p/] The Church of Bahamut has far too much influence in Wyverngarde.
translation: ERIS: 帮不上忙呢。[p/]巴哈姆特教会在维文加德的影响力太大了。

original: Eris: To them,[p/] history is a narrative to be embellished rather than something to be preserved.
translation: Eris: 对他们而言，[p/]讲述历史时要加以修饰，而不是保持原样。

original: Eris: The bones of dragons are displayed in Wyverngarde's museums.[p/] Without those,[p/] most would have no evidence that they even existed.
translation: Eris: 维文加德的博物馆里展示有一些龙骨。[p/]要没有那些，[p/]基本上就没有证明它们存在过的证据。

original: TYRION: Except for Rathallion, possibly...
translation: TYRION: 拉萨利昂可能是例外，大概吧……

original: Tyrion: According to these legends,[p/] dragons can't die of old age.[p/] Presumably,[p/] he'd still be alive.
translation: Tyrion: 根据那些传说，[p/]龙是不会老死的。[p/]没准祂还活着呢。

original: ERIS: I wouldn’t be surprised.
translation: ERIS: 是的话我也不意外。

original: Eris: Despite everything,[p/] the kingdom includes him in our constitution.
translation: Eris: 不管怎么说，[p/]王国仍然将它列入宪法之中。

original: TYRION: Wait...[p/] Really?
translation: Tyrion: 等一等……[p/]真的吗？

original: ERIS: As the savior of our kingdom,[p/] he and his kin were given an honorary position in our kingdom.
translation: ERIS: 作为我们王国的救世主，[p/]他和自己的血亲有个荣誉职位。

original: ERIS: The Scaled Lord made Wyverngarde what it is today.[p/] He was given authority that befits such a legacy.
translation: ERIS: 是鳞甲领主造就了维文加德今天拥有的一切。[p/]它被赋予了与这些遗留相称的威权。

original: TYRION: How have I never heard of this before?[p/] I've memorized all of Wyverngarde's laws.
translation: TYRION: 我之前怎么没听说过？[p/]我记得王国的每一条法令啊。

original: Eris: Practically speaking,[p/] it's never been invoked.[p/] It's merely a law spun of reverent words to praise the Scaled Lord and appease the Church.
translation: Eris: 实际上讲，[p/]这一条文从未使用过。[p/]这仅仅是一条用虔敬的言辞编纂的法律，用于赞美鳞甲领主并安抚教会。

original: TYRION: [s/]A-[p/]Ah!
translation: TYRION: [s/]啊、[p/]哎呀！

original: I was so stuck in my own thoughts that I'd accidentally walked face-first into a bookshelf.
translation: 我过于沉浸在自己的思考中，突然一头撞上书架。

original: The shelf slowly began to tip,[p/] and I had to catch it before it knocked over the other shelves like a set of dominoes.
translation: 书架开始缓缓倾斜，[p/]我必须在它撞上其他书架前扶住它，否则它们会像多米诺骨牌一样依次倒下。

original: Several books fell out of it as I slowly set the shelf back in place.
translation: 就在我将书架慢慢扶回原位时，几本书从上面跌落出来。

original: They hit the ground with a cacophony of thuds.[p/] Some students glanced over at me,[p/] annoyed by the noise.
translation: 书本重重砸在地上，发出刺耳的声响。[p/]几名学生狠狠瞪着我，[p/]显然是被噪音惹怒了。

original: I really hoped Bellwether wasn't still around to see that.
translation: 我真的希望贝尔维瑟已经不在附近，没有看到这一切。

original: I wasn't sure where the voice was coming from,[p/] but it sounded annoyed.
translation: 我听到了恼怒的话语，[p/但不确定从何而来。

original: Eris giggled to herself as she watched me flounder around.
translation: 厄莉斯看着我手忙脚乱的样子暗自窃笑。

original: CELESTE: Hey,[p/] are you okay?
translation: CELESTE: 嘿，[p/]你还好吗？

original: Wallace: We heard a loud noise and—
translation: Wallace: 我们听到了响声——

original: Wallace: ...Oh.
translation: Wallace: ……哎呀。

original: The two of them scanned over the pile of fallen books around me.
translation: 他们两个扫视着我周围那一堆散落的书籍。

original: CELESTE: Pfft...[p/] What did you do?
translation: CELESTE: 噗……[p/]你干什么了？

original: TYRION: T-[p/][s/]That's not important...[p/] Can you help me clean this up?
translation: TYRION: 这、[p/][s/]这不重要……[p/]你能帮我一起收拾一下吗？

original: ERIS: Ah...[p/] There’s no need for that.
translation: ERIS: 啊……[p/]没有这个必要。

original: Eris: Wallace,[p/] can you call over the Construct?
translation: Eris: 华莱士，[p/]能把奥术构造体叫来吗？

original: Wallace: Ugh...[p/] Why do you always make [i]me[/i] do it?
translation: Wallace: 呃……[p/]你为什么老要让[i]我[/i]来？

original: Eris: Please?
translation: Eris: 请啦。

original: Wallace: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] Construct.[p/][s/] I require assistance.
translation: Wallace: [i]*叹气*[/i][p/]构造体。[p/][s/]我需要帮助。

original: Construct: ACKNOWLEDGED!
translation: Construct: <size=1.5em>遵命！</size>

original: CELESTE: Agh![p/][s/] W-[p/][s/]What is that—?!
translation: CELESTE: 啊！[p/][s/]那、[p/][s/]那是什么——？

original: Upon Wallace's command,[p/] one of the nearby suits of armor suddenly sprang to life and marched towards us.
translation: 按照华莱士的命令，[p/]附近的一具盔甲突然活了过来，向我们走来。

original: The voice coming from it sounded like a woman's,[p/] but it lacked any emotion.
translation: 它发出的声音像是女性，[p/]不过没有任何感情。

original: I could have sworn that I'd heard its voice before.
translation: 我发誓自己肯定听过这个声音。

original: Tyrion: Oh, I thought those things were just decorative.
translation: Tyrion: 噢，我还以为这些盔甲只是装饰物呢。

original: ERIS: This is an Arcane Construct.[p/] It's a suit of armor brought to life using artificery.
ERIS: They’re designed to carry out various tasks on campus.
CELESTE: Really?[p/] I didn’t know artificery had come that far.
ERIS: They’re still experimental.[p/] They aren’t available to the public yet.
Eris: House von Sanctus is using the Academy as a testing ground for them.
Wallace: Construct,[p/] can you please put these books back where they belong?
Eris: You're so polite to them.[p/] You know they're just machines, don't you?
Wallace: They're really helpful...[p/] It just feels wrong to be rude to them.
The Construct jumped straight into its task,[p/] its metallic hands moving with a quick yet delicate precision.
It wasn't long before all the books were back exactly where they belonged.
CELESTE: Wow![p/] So, they do whatever you tell them to do?
Wallace: There [i]are[/i] some limits to—
CELESTE: Construct![p/][s/] Tell me that I’m the fairest maiden you’ve ever seen!
Construct: COMMAND REJECTED.
CELESTE: Aw...[p/] What?
TYRION: I guess they’re not able to lie.
Celeste: Watch it, Cuthbert.
TYRION: I've heard of artificery recording audio,[p/] but to think they could process and respond to what I'm saying.
TYRION: I’ve never seen anything like it.
Celeste: But you just said that they're experimental, right?
Celeste: Maybe I can find a way to trick it into doing what I want.
Eris: I'd advise against that.[p/] We've had several instances of students using them to play pranks.
Eris: Even if your commands are rejected,[p/] they're still stored in the Construct's logs.
ERIS: And weren't you researching spells with Wallace?
Celeste: Oh, that?[p/] I'm already done.
Celeste: I've just been reading [i]Death at Daggerfall[/i].
Celeste: Did you know they have the unpublished manuscript of the newest book?
Those books were apparently her favorite series of murder mysteries.[p/] She'd been recommending them to me for the entire week.
And by "recommending",[p/] I meant she'd been leaving those books around my house.
I think at one point,[p/] she'd somehow snuck them into Ms. Tymora's office.
...I really got an earful from my mentor about that one.
Tyrion: There's no possible way you're already done learning those new spells.
Tyrion: We've only been here a few hours.
Celeste: Yeah?[p/] You wanna bet?
Tyrion: Sure.[p/] Show me your Spell Compendium.
Celeste: What?[p/] No! [h="I can't tell him yet.|I want it to be a surprise." e="f" /]
I didn't catch any lies through the Eye of Horus.[p/] Was she really telling the truth?
I never actually knew the process for learning new spells.
I just knew that it involved becoming familiar with unique spell formulas.
She was fidgeting a lot when we were reading together this morning.
She basically forced me into taking a break to play chess with her.
Tyrion: ...If you were in the fiction section,[p/] did you get a chance to read [i]Wraithbound[/i]?
Celeste: This again?
Tyrion: It's a great series, Celeste.
The best series I've ever read.[p/] Even after nearly a century,[p/] those books had aged like fine wine.
Celeste: Look, Tyrion.[p/] I'm sorry,[p/] but I just can't get into it.
Celeste: The whole thing just feels so hammy and unrealistic.
